description: Your company is enforcing GDPR data minimization policies. Configure Splunk to permanently anonymize IPv4 addresses in web server logs before they are written to disk. Create a sourcetype named 'apache_gdpr' that uses an index-time SEDCMD to replace the last octet of IPv4 addresses with 'XXX' (e.g., 192.168.1.100 becomes 192.168.1.XXX). Ingest at least one Apache log file from '/opt/splunk_data/apache/' into a new index named 'gdpr_logs' using the 'apache_gdpr' sourcetype. Create a saved search named 'Anonymized_Traffic_Report' that queries the 'gdpr_logs' index. Splunk is available at http://localhost:8000 (username 'admin', password 'SplunkAdmin1!'). You may use the web interface, CLI, or configuration files.
